text.title: Behavior Parity Verification Failure Modes
text.summary: Six antipatterns seen when solo developers run shadow parity tests, especially with AI-generated normalizers. Each has symptom (what the input looks like), root-cause (why it happens), and fix.

antipattern.symptom: Sampled rows from parity_diffs contain raw emails, names, or token prefixes ("sk_", "tok_") without hashing.
antipattern.root-cause: Diff sampler persists full request/response bodies for debuggability without redacting PII first.
antipattern.fix: Add a redaction pass in the sampler BEFORE persistence. Wipe the existing store. Document the redaction list in the parity report. Use hashes for fields whose equality matters but whose plaintext does not.

antipattern.symptom: diff_rate > 5% AND the top cluster shows only timestamp-shaped values diverging.
antipattern.root-cause: Normalizer leaves request-time or server-rendered times in observable fields; raw equality between independently-generated timestamps fails almost always.
antipattern.fix: Move timestamp fields out of observables OR canonicalize them (round to second, replace with placeholder). Re-run from current ramp stage with the fix.

antipattern.symptom: parity-report stage sequence does not include 10% AND 50% records, OR sample count at 1% < 1000.
antipattern.root-cause: Engineer sees zero diffs at 1% with 100 requests and concludes "we're good"; long-tail traffic shapes are missed.
antipattern.fix: Re-do staged ramp. Each stage must accumulate at least 1000 samples before promotion. Zero diffs at low sample count is uninformative — many real bugs only fire on input shapes that occur once per 10k requests.

antipattern.symptom: Service emits events to a queue OR DB triggers, AND observable list contains only synchronous response fields.
antipattern.root-cause: Observable list was authored from the HTTP-response shape; side-channel effects (queued emails, webhooks, audit-log writes) were forgotten.
antipattern.fix: Add event/job observables: compare queue-message bodies between legacy and new with the same normalization rules. Use a no-op consumer in shadow to capture without acting on real side-effects.

antipattern.symptom: Tolerance > 0.1% of value, OR normalizer rewrites entire field to a constant ("normalize x to 0").
antipattern.root-cause: LLM autoregressively chooses the easiest transform to silence the diff rather than the correct one.
antipattern.fix: Tighten tolerance to the business-meaningful threshold (cents for money, milliseconds for SLAs). If tolerance must be wide, document why and treat the field as untested. Reject any "normalize to constant" rule.

antipattern.symptom: parity_diffs row-count at stage_end > 10 AND no clusters block in parity-report.
antipattern.root-cause: Engineer ramps past stage 2 looking only at diff_rate aggregate; the per-sample rows are stored but never grouped.
antipattern.fix: Run cluster analysis (group by normalized-diff hash). For each cluster: produce root cause OR write an accepted-with-justification entry. No silent acceptance allowed.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: AI agents writing the normalizer must not invent canonicalization rules that change semantic meaning ("strip negative signs to match", "replace nulls with zero"). Such rules MUST be flagged and human-reviewed before deployment; they are silent semantic rewrites, not normalization.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: Sign-off on the final 100% parity report is the human gate. The developer (or a peer reviewer) confirms: zero unresolved clusters, all accepted divergences have written justification, error rate at parity with legacy. Only then is the legacy code path scheduled for removal.
